subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Wildfire Behavior
description: A quick, practical guide to Wildfire Behavior.

hook.text: A wildfire can create its own weather system, generating lightning that starts new fires miles away.

buildup.text: Wildfires need fuel, oxygen, and heat—the fire triangle. Remove one and the fire dies.

discovery.text: Fire travels uphill faster because heat rises and preheats the fuel above—doubling speed per slope.

twist.text: Embers can travel over a mile ahead of the fire front, igniting new fires behind evacuation routes.

climax.text: Creating defensible space—clearing brush 30 feet from your home—dramatically improves survival.

punchline.text: Fire makes its own wind. Its own weather. Respect it or flee.

quiz.question: Why do wildfires travel faster uphill?
options: Rising heat preheats the fuel above
There is more oxygen at higher elevations
